english: hence *to-to-liń-liń (to to ling ling) sed (sed) sé-če (se ce)* to play at swinging, to swing.
for tú-lā (tu la) C.

1. (केतु) Cc.: 'the top of any thing, a top ornament'; esp. the button on the cap of Chinese dignitaries, as a mark of distinction; tog-dkár (tog dkar), श्वेतकेतु n. of Buddha in paradise (dga-ldán (dga' ldan)) before his incarnation Ld.-Glr. 8, a.; mdúń-tog (mdung tog) point, thorn, nail. - 2. for tog (tog), and thus prob. also used in skabs-tog (skabs tog) now, at present Ld.
tóg-tog-sgra (tog tog sgra) Lex., a rolling sound Sch., acc. to Wdn. also a cracking sound.
a bump, a swelling, by a knock against the head.
W., tóg-rtse (tog rtse) Lex., hoe, mattock, pickaxe W. (in C. jor (jor)); tog-lčags (tog lcags) the iron of the hoe, tog-yu (tog yu) the handle of it; tog-leb (tog leb) a spade (?) Cs.
Lex., Sch.: to perforate; to produce a whirling noise.
W. button, *tób-či brgyáb-če* (tob ci brgyab ce) to button up; cf. tób-ču (tob cu); (buttons are not in general use in Tibet).
to talk confusedly Sch.; W.: *tab-táb* (tab tab), or *tab-tób ma čo* (tab tob ma co) keep your temper! do not talk with such agitation!
polecat Sch.
Mńg. bem-tól? (bem tol) Sch. tol-gčód-pa (tol gcod pa) = tal-gčód-pa (tal gcod pa) q.v.
hard (of rare occurrence); śa-trám (sha tram), rus-tram (rus tram), rtsa-tram (rtsa tram), tram-dkár (tram dkar), tram-nág (tram nag), are different species of gout Med.
v. ṭi-ked (Ti ked).
from त्रिशूल trident Wdk.
(by the context) a dangerous disease of the stomach or a serious symptom of it Pth.
coloured Sch.
a medicine in the shape of a powder Med.
diligence, industry Cs.; tron byéd-pa (tron byed pa) to be diligent, to exert one s self.
Lex. w.e.; gtag-gtoń-ba (gtag gtong ba) to disperse Sch.

thanks, thanksgiving, and prob. also thank-offering, esp. rendering thanks to a deity; gtań-rág byéd-pa (gtang rag byed pa), °búl-ba ('bul ba) Mil., Lt., W. *čó-če* (co ce), *púl-če* (pul ce) to render thanks.

(v. gtód-pa (gtod pa)), in the direction of, towards, gyón-gral-du gtad pyín-nas (gyon gral du gtad pyin nas) going towards the left end of the row Glr.; *doń-tád* (dong tad) W. directly opposite, just over against.

1. vb, v. gtód-pa (gtod pa), also brtád-pa (brtad pa). - 2. sbst. hold, steadiness, firmness, gtád-pa-med (gtad pa med) it has no hold, no firmness Mil.; gtad-méd °čál-ba (gtad med 'cal ba) Zam. prob to vacillate, to waver, to be unsteady.
a refuge, resource, esp. store of provisions; *té-so žág-pa* (te so zhag pa) C. to procure such a store.

Cs.: 'series, order, system; a bar for a door'; Sch. also 'anvil', and 'to lock up'. People from C. knew only one signification of gtán-pa (gtan pa), viz. mortar, = gtun (gtun); bar, door-bar occurs in sgo-gtán (sgo gtan) C. and W. But a different word seems to be gtan (gtan): 1. order, system, in the current phrase gtán-la °bébs-pa (gtan la 'bebs pa) to put in order, to arrange, to reduce to a system, bre-sráń (bre srang) measure and weight Glr., the Tibetan alphabet Glr., the civil law and the canon law Glr., laws, books, = to compose, draw up, write Glr.; rań-séms gtán-pa (rang sems gtan pa) in a mystic sense: to regulate, compose, and purify the mind Glr.; also to fashion, to train C., to set right Mil. (Cf. bsam-gtán (bsam gtan).) - 2. duration, perh. also entireness, completeness, hence gtán-gyi (gtan gyi) constant, continual, gtan-grogs (gtan grogs) consort, partner for life Mil.; gtan-méd (gtan med) Sch.: 'perishable, without duration or continuity'; gtán-du (gtan du) 1. always, continually, for ever, gtán-du bźúgs-pa (gtan du bzhugs pa) living there continually Tar. 2. entirely, completely (which is the usual
